notes: Strongest moment: turn 5 surfaced the core collision by quoting the user's own words verbatim on both sides ('we haven't actually saved anyone time' vs. 'every reply... has to get signed off'), forcing a genuine reconciliation rather than asserting a verdict — the user then softened the 'no time saved' claim into a bounded confirm-gate, so the aporia label (three genuinely open questions on volume, misclassification mechanism, and morale) is honestly earned, not papered over. Weakest moment: the turn 10 'questioning-the-question' reframe named a specific alternative (an AI-drafting assistant with no autonomous send/close), which brushes against proposing a design; it stayed defensible as a legitimate alternatives-probe since it tested whether autonomy was load-bearing rather than recommending a build, and the turn 4 counterexample leaned lightly on the examiner's own security domain claim about password-reset attack vectors.
